text:
Market today: A mixed mood persists with concerns about the AI trade and a potential shift away from mega-cap tech. Treasury yields are up, but a positive note for chips comes from Bezos backing CuspAI and Nvidia. Your tech/chip heavy book is directly in the crosshairs of these conflicting signals.

Your holdings: SOXL (-46.77%) and SPAL (-47.83%) are showing significant losses, and their listed stops are now above current prices, needing immediate review. QQQ (-2.7%), SMH (-12.55%), and QQQI (-3.9%) are on SELL status and nearing their stops. Remember, margin amplifies these drawdowns.

Watch: Keep an eye on the broader chip sector for clarity, given the divided Wall Street opinions and the "Magnificent Seven" talk. Specifically, monitor your existing tech ETFs around their stops.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your concentrated, leveraged positions amidst uncertain tech sector sentiment.
plays: SOXL & SPAL: Your listed stops for these leveraged ETFs (SOXL 200, SPAL 21.38) are above current prices. Re-evaluate these stops immediately to manage risk on these significant losses.
QQQ, SMH, QQQI: These are on SELL status and approaching their stops (QQQ 670, SMH 540, QQQI 52). Be prepared to trim if these levels are breached to protect capital.
NVDA: Consider a new long entry given the positive Bezos/CuspAI news. Scanner shows entry 202.46-202.11, stop 200.13.
